mechanism: Hand-cranked pinwheel (Odhner) with sliding-pin setting wheels. One addition or subtraction per crank revolution; multiplication is repeated addition with carriage shifts (a 3-digit multiply takes ~17 crank turns). No stored program -- register-based base-10 arithmetic.

historical_notes.claim: A full multiplication takes about 17 crank turns on a pinwheel calculator.
Original-Odhner large-register models (20-digit result register) date to 1940-1954.

usable_assumptions.confidence: low -- one add per crank rev at ~1 rev/s; operator-dependent
